name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist and materials scientist with 6+ years of experience synthesizing, processing, and characterizing functional material systems, using spectroscopy, thermal analysis, electrochemistry, CVD/surface preparation, and DOE to connect process conditions with structure-property-performance. Currently leading polymer process development at CU Boulder while bringing prior thin-film coatings, interface, and data-driven experimental design experience from Georgia Tech and ETH Zurich.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead process-development experiments for bio-based polymer systems, using DOE to define synthesis/process windows, improve reproducibility, and translate material-performance targets into executable experimental plans.
Build multi-technique characterization workflows using FT-IR, thermal analysis, and spectroscopy to connect processing decisions with material structure, quality, and performance outcomes.

Designed and synthesized functional organic electrochromic material systems, then established structure-property relationships through FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, electrochemical, TGA, and DSC characterization.
Investigated degradation, adhesion, and interface-instability mechanisms in thin-film systems, converting failure-analysis results into material design and process recommendations.

Co-led AFOSR-funded development of molecular thin-film coatings for optical hardware, designing surface-preparation and CVD deposition strategies to tune morphology, adhesion, and device-relevant performance.
Developed and optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring, establishing process controls that improved coating reproducibility and uniformity.

Managed a 15-member research laboratory operating multiple analytical instruments and parallel materials projects, implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems, and improved instrument uptime by 30%.
Authored SOPs and coordinated technical workflows across concurrent projects with tight quality and timeline requirements.

leadership.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
bullets: Mentored 120+ undergraduates and developed supplemental teaching materials that improved exam performance by 15%.

projects.title: Functional Materials Synthesis & Characterization
projects.description: Synthesized and characterized electrochromic materials, linking molecular design and processing decisions to optical, electrochemical, thermal, and stability performance.
projects.tech: Organic synthesis, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, electrochemistry, TGA, DSC

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Developed molecular thin-film coatings for optical hardware, tuning CVD and surface-preparation conditions to achieve target morphology, adhesion, and performance.
projects.tech: CVD, surface chemistry, coatings, materials characterization

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Lead DOE-guided process-development experiments for bio-based polymer systems, improving reproducibility, material quality, and translation from target properties to experimental plans.
projects.tech: DOE, process optimization, polymer chemistry, spectroscopy

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Materials Synthesis & Characterization: Functional organic materials, polymer systems, thin films, coatings, CVD, surface preparation, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, ellipsometry, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, NMR, spectroelectrochemistry
skills.Structure-Property Analysis: Structure-property-processing relationships, degradation analysis, adhesion/interface troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, data interpretation
skills.Process & Data: DOE, SPC, statistical data analysis, process optimization, technical documentation, experimental planning
skills.Execution & Communication: Multi-project management, cross-functional communication, stakeholder updates, scientific writing
